results.title: AI Meeting Notes (beta) – Notion Help Center
excerpts:
Section Title: AI Meeting Notes (beta) > Who can use AI Meeting Notes?
Content:
AI Meeting Notes is available on the Notion app and browser. The Notion app provides the best experience, especially for video calls, because it captures all sides of the conversation using your system audio and mic. The browser is best suited for in-person conversations since it can only pick up audio from your microphone.
If you're using this feature on your browser, a few things to note:
Only mic inputs will be transcribed. System audio won't be captured.
Audio from conferencing tools (Google Meet, Zoom, Microsoft Teams, etc) will not be captured if you are using headphones. If you’re not using headphones and your device’s volume isn’t too low, audio from conferencing tools may be captured.

results.title: AI notetaker implementation guide: Setup, adoption & change management for CS teams | Granola
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > Step 2: Tool selection
Content:
| Factor | Granola | Otter.ai | Fireflies.ai |
| Capture method | Device audio, no visible participant | Bot joins meeting | Bot joins meeting |
| Recording announcement | None | Reportedly "Recording started" | Bot joins as participant |
| Setup time | Under 5 minutes | Under 10 minutes | Under 10 minutes |
| HubSpot integration | Yes (Business plan) | Limited | Yes |
| Slack integration | Yes (Business plan) | Reportedly yes | Reportedly yes |
| Privacy architecture | Audio deleted after transcription | Audio stored | Audio stored |
| SOC 2 Type 2 | Yes (July 2025) | Yes | Yes |
| GDPR | Yes | Reportedly yes | Reportedly yes |
| AI training opt-out | Yes, all plans (off by default on Enterprise) | Reportedly available | Reportedly available |
| Business plan pricing | $14/user/month | $20/user/month | $19/user/month |
| Audio playback | No | Yes | Yes |
| Conversation analytics (talk ratios, sentiment) | No | Limited | Yes |
 ... 
Section Title: ... > How Granola helps CS teams capture client calls
Content:
CS teams report concerns about visible AI bots on client calls because recording announcements can shift conversation dynamics. Granola captures device audio directly, so there's [no visible participant](https://docs.granola.ai/help-center/consent-security-privacy/in-meeting-notice-google-meet) in the meeting list and no recording announcement. The meeting dynamic doesn't change.
That said, letting participants know notes are being taken remains good practice regardless of the tooling: a brief mention at the start of the call keeps the relationship transparent and the conversation on solid ground.
Granola doesn't offer audio playback or conversation analytics like talk-time ratios and sentiment scoring. If your CS team needs call coaching workflows built around those features, a dedicated conversation intelligence tool is a better fit for that use case.
 ... 
Section Title: ... > Key terminology
Content:
**Bot-free capture:** Capturing meeting audio through device audio rather than a third-party participant that joins the video call. No participant list entry, no recording announcement, and no change to the meeting dynamic for other attendees.
**AI-enhanced notes:** The workflow where a CSM jots rough notes during a meeting and AI additions appear distinctly from the CSM's own input, filling in context, quotes, and detail from the transcript. The CSM controls what stays and what gets removed.
**Institutional memory:** The accumulated knowledge captured across all client conversations, preserved in searchable team folders that survive individual employee departures and can be queried for patterns across accounts.
**Folder-level queries:** The ability to ask a question across all meetings in a shared folder simultaneously, returning source-linked citations from specific calls rather than a generic summary.

Section Title: Enable call recording in Attio
Content:
Set up the Attio call recorder to record meetings for playback, transcripts, and insights.
Table of Contents
Compatible video conferencing platforms Languages supported for call transcription Manage auto-record settings Customize call recorder appearance FAQ
Available on pro and enterprise plans.
Available for **all** workspace members.
Attio’s call recorder seamlessly integrates with your video conferencing platform to capture meetings for easy sharing with your team. Learn how to enable call recording in Attio to utilize AI-generated insight summaries and transcripts and ensure key insights about your relationships are accessible and actionable.
Section Title: Enable call recording in Attio > **Compatible video conferencing platforms** Copy link
Content:
The Attio call recorder can be used with the following video conferencing platforms:
Zoom
Google Meets
Microsoft Teams
The Attio call recorder works with these platforms out of the box, unless default settings have been changed on Zoom or Microsoft Teams. Learn how to troubleshoot Zoom settings and Micosoft Teams settings .
Each member of your workspace can manage their auto-record settings and choose whether to admit or deny the Attio call recorder at the start of each meeting.
**Note:** In order for the recorder to join a Zoom call and start recording, it needs to be granted access into the call by the meeting host or creator.

Section Title: Recording calls with Attio > Admit, deny, or stop recordings during meetings Copy link
Content:
For example, any participant can send any of the following messages in the chat to remove Attio from the call and stop recording on Zoom, Google Meet, and Microsoft Teams:
"@Attio leave"
"/bot leave"
"Please leave the call Attio"
"Remove the note taker"
You can only ask Attio to leave after it has been admitted to the call.

results.title: Troubleshooting call recording in Attio | Attio Help Center
excerpts:
Section Title: Troubleshooting call recording in Attio
Content:
Find solutions for when calls aren't being recorded as expected.
Table of Contents
Identify the issue Still need help?
If the Attio call recorder isn’t working as expected, there are a few common issues you can check.
This article covers:
Why the recorder may not be joining calls
Why calls may not be recorded successfully
How to resolve common issues with Zoom, Microsoft Teams, and Google Meet
Why the recorder may be joining unexpectedly
 ... 
Section Title: Troubleshooting call recording in Attio > ... > Troubleshooting Zoom settings Copy link
Content:
Zoom settings may need to be adjusted for Attio to successfully record calls and generate transcripts. In addition, the Zoom host must attend the meeting.
**Configure caption settings**
Go to [Zoom settings](https://zoom.us/profile/setting?tab=meeting) .
Turn on **Automated captions** .
If transcripts are still not being generated:
Hover over **Show captions** and click the ^ icon.
Select **Host caption control settings** .
Enable **Allow closed captioning for this meeting** .
If you don’t see an option to enable meeting captions, it’s likely the global **Automated captions** setting is turned off in your Zoom account settings.
**Configure local recording settings**
Local recording must be enabled for Attio to record successfully.
 ... 
Section Title: Troubleshooting call recording in Attio > ... > Troubleshooting Microsoft Teams settings Copy link
Content:
Microsoft Teams settings may need to be adjusted for Attio to successfully record calls and generate transcripts.
Ensure [live captions](https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/use-live-captions-in-microsoft-teams-meetings-4be2d304-f675-4b57-8347-cbd000a21260) are enabled in your meeting:
Go to **More actions** .
Select **Language and speech** .
Select **Show live captions** .
Captions may also be disabled by Microsoft admins. Admins can check [this setting](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoftteams/meeting-transcription-captions) to confirm.
 ... 
Section Title: ... > The call is recorded, but transcripts aren't correct Copy link
Content:
If the call is not in English, confirm that the call has ended. The transcript is generated in a single supported language after the call ends.
You can manually regenerate any call transcript within seven days of the call. Click the ⋮ icon in the top-right of a call page, then go to **Transcript** > **Regenerate** .
 ... 
Section Title: Troubleshooting call recording in Attio > Still need help? Copy link
Content:
If you’ve worked through the steps above and Attio still isn’t recording calls as expected, contact support at support@attio.com .
To enable quicker troubleshooting, please include:
The meeting link
The time and date of the call
The conferencing platform used
What you observed (recorder didn’t join, joined but no recording, transcript issue, etc.)

results.title: Attio AI Meeting Assistant: Syncing Meetings into Attio CRM - Klu
excerpts:
Section Title: Attio AI Meeting Assistant: Syncing Meetings into Attio CRM
Content:
3 Oct 2025 • 7 minute read
Sami AZ
[Attio](https://klu.so/integrations/crm/attio) is positioning itself as a modern, flexible CRM with strong automation capabilities. In 2025, it offers *“Call Intelligence”* as a native feature: automatic recording, transcripts, summaries, and insights built directly into the CRM.
Yet there is still a gap: turning that meeting data into structured workflows, tasks, and CRM actions that scale across teams. That is what a full **Attio AI meeting assistant** should deliver, differentiating from just a record-and-transcribe layer.
In this article, we will:
Explain how Attio’s built-in Call Intelligence feature works
Survey third-party integrations that sync meetings into Attio CRM
Highlight strengths and limitations of current options
Define what next-level integration should include
Show where Klu’s architecture can lead with automation, reliability, and workflow depth
Section Title: ... > Attio’s Native Call Intelligence: What It Offers
Content:
Attio’s built-in Call Intelligence is already powerful. Here’s what it supports:
Automatic recording of meetings across Zoom, Google Meet, and Microsoft Teams.
Real-time transcription with speaker labels and timestamps.
Insight templates and AI summaries generated for recorded calls.
Ability to view recordings and transcripts in multiple places in Attio: the Calls tab, Activity tab on records, and a Calls page listing all recordings.
Linking recorded calls automatically (or manually) to contact, company, or meeting records in Attio.
The ability to override auto-record behavior on specific meetings (e.g. force record on/off).
Attio’s built-in call intelligence turns meetings into searchable insights inside the CRM.
While this native feature is strong, there remain practical limitations when scaling for teams that need automation beyond insight display.
Section Title: ... > Attio’s Native Call Intelligence: What It Offers
Content:
Read more about our article [Why Transcription Alone Is Dead in the Age of AI](https://klu.so/blog/why-transcription-alone-is-dead) ?
Section Title: ... > Third-Party Meeting → Attio Integrations
Content:
Several integrations and apps already connect meeting tools to Attio CRM. Let’s review who is doing what, and how deep.
